    19	def get_ping_body_bytes(ping: Ping | None) -> bytes | None:
    20	    """Return ping body as bytes for a given Ping object.
    21	
    22	    If body has not been uploaded to object storage yet, wait 5 seconds
    23	    and try to fetch it again.
    24	    """
    25	    if ping and ping.has_body():
    26	        try:
    27	            if result := ping.get_body_bytes():
    28	                return result
    29	
    30	            if ping.object_size:
    31	                # If the ping object has an object size but get_body_bytes returns
    32	                # None then body is not uploaded to the object storage yet.
    33	                # When sending notifications we can afford to wait a little
    34	                # bit and retry.
    35	                time.sleep(5)
    36	                return ping.get_body_bytes()
    37	        except Ping.GetBodyError:
    38	            pass
    39	
    40	    return None
    41	
    42	
    43	def get_ping_body(ping: Ping | None, maxlen: int | None = None) -> str | None:
    44	    """Return ping body for a given Ping object."""
    45	    body = None
    46	    if body_bytes := get_ping_body_bytes(ping):
    47	        body = body_bytes.decode(errors="replace")
    48	        if maxlen and len(body) > maxlen:
    49	            body = body[:maxlen] + "\n[truncated]"
    50	
    51	    return body

    54	class TransportError(Exception):
    55	    def __init__(self, message: str, permanent: bool = False) -> None:
    56	        self.message = message
    57	        self.permanent = permanent

    64	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    65	        """Send notification about current status of the check.
    66	
    67	        This method raises TransportError on error, and returns None
    68	        on success.
    69	
    70	        """
    71	
    72	        raise NotImplementedError()
    73	
    74	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    75	        """Return True if transport will ignore check's current status.
    76	
    77	        This method is overridden in Webhook subclass where the user can
    78	        configure webhook urls for "up" and "down" events, and both are
    79	        optional.
    80	
    81	        """
    82	
    83	        return False
    84	
    85	    def down_checks(self, check: Check) -> list[Check] | None:
    86	        """Return a sorted list of other checks in the same project that are down.
    87	
    88	        If there are no other hecks in the project, return None instead of empty list.
    89	        Templates can check for None to decide whether to show or not show the
    90	        "All other checks are up" note.
    91	
    92	        """
    93	
    94	        siblings = self.channel.project.check_set.exclude(id=check.id)
    95	        if not siblings.exists():
    96	            return None
    97	
    98	        down_siblings = list(siblings.filter(status="down"))
    99	        sortchecks(down_siblings, "name")
   100	
   101	        return down_siblings

   103	    def last_ping(self, flip: Flip) -> Ping | None:
   104	        """Return the last Ping object received before this flip."""
   105	
   106	        if not flip.owner.pk:
   107	            return None
   108	
   109	        # Sort by "created". Sorting by "id" can cause postgres to pick api_ping.id
   110	        # index (slow if the api_ping table is big)
   111	        q = flip.owner.ping_set.order_by("created")
   112	        # Make sure we're not selecting pings that occurred after the flip
   113	        q = q.filter(created__lte=flip.created)
   114	
   115	        return q.last()

   117	    def tmpl(self, template_name: str, **ctx: Any) -> str:
   118	        # \xa0 is non-breaking space. It causes SMS messages to use UCS2 encoding

   130	class HttpTransport(Transport):
   131	    @classmethod
   132	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
   133	        # Subclasses can override this method to produce a more specific message.
   134	        raise TransportError(f"Received status code {response.status_code}")
   135	
   136	    @classmethod
   137	    def _request(
   138	        cls,
   139	        method: str,
   140	        url: str,
   141	        *,
   142	        params: curl.Params,
   143	        data: curl.Data,
   144	        json: Any,
   145	        headers: curl.Headers,
   146	        auth: curl.Auth,
   147	    ) -> None:
   148	        try:
   149	            r = curl.request(
   150	                method,
   151	                url,
   152	                params=params,
   153	                data=data,
   154	                json=json,
   155	                headers=headers,
   156	                auth=auth,
   157	                timeout=30,
   158	            )
   159	            if r.status_code not in (200, 201, 202, 204):
   160	                cls.raise_for_response(r)
   161	        except curl.CurlError as e:
   162	            raise TransportError(e.message)
   163	
   164	    @classmethod
   165	    def request(
   166	        cls,
   167	        method: str,
   168	        url: str,
   169	        *,
   170	        retry: bool,
   171	        params: curl.Params = None,
   172	        data: curl.Data = None,
   173	        json: Any = None,
   174	        headers: curl.Headers = None,
   175	        auth: curl.Auth = None,
   176	    ) -> None:
   177	        tries_left = 3 if retry else 1
   178	        while True:
   179	            try:
   180	                return cls._request(
   181	                    method,
   182	                    url,
   183	                    params=params,
   184	                    data=data,
   185	                    json=json,
   186	                    headers=headers,
   187	                    auth=auth,
   188	                )
   189	            except TransportError as e:
   190	                tries_left = 0 if e.permanent else tries_left - 1
   191	                # If we have no tries left then abort the retry loop by re-raising
   192	                # the exception:
   193	                if tries_left == 0:
   194	                    raise e
   195	
   196	    # Convenience wrapper around self.request for making "POST" requests
   197	    @classmethod
   198	    def post(
   199	        cls,
   200	        url: str,
   201	        retry: bool = True,
   202	        *,
   203	        params: curl.Params = None,
   204	        data: curl.Data = None,
   205	        json: Any = None,
   206	        headers: curl.Headers = None,
   207	        auth: curl.Auth = None,
   208	    ) -> None:
   209	        cls.request(
   210	            "post",
   211	            url,
   212	            retry=retry,
   213	            params=params,
   214	            data=data,
   215	            json=json,
   216	            headers=headers,
   217	            auth=auth,
   218	        )
   219	
   220	    # Convenience wrapper around self.request for making "PUT" requests
   221	    @classmethod
   222	    def put(
   223	        cls,
   224	        url: str,
   225	        retry: bool = True,
   226	        *,
   227	        params: curl.Params = None,
   228	        data: curl.Data = None,
   229	        json: Any = None,
   230	        headers: curl.Headers = None,
   231	        auth: curl.Auth = None,
   232	    ) -> None:
   233	        cls.request(
   234	            "put",
   235	            url,
   236	            retry=retry,
   237	            params=params,
   238	            data=data,
   239	            json=json,
   240	            headers=headers,
   241	            auth=auth,
   242	        )

   152	@dataclass
   153	class DowntimeRecord:
   154	    boundary: datetime  # The start of this time interval (timezone-aware)
   155	    tz: str  # For calculating total seconds in a month
   156	    no_data: bool  # True if the check did not yet exist in this time interval
   157	    duration: td  # Total downtime in this time interval
   158	    count: int  # The number of downtime events in this time interval
   159	
   160	    def monthly_uptime(self) -> float:
   161	        # NB: this method assumes monthly boundaries.
   162	        # It will yield incorrect results for weekly boundaries
   163	        max_seconds = seconds_in_month(self.boundary.date(), self.tz)
   164	        up_seconds = max_seconds - self.duration.total_seconds()
   165	        return up_seconds / max_seconds
   166	
   167	
   168	class DowntimeRecorder:
   169	    def __init__(self, boundaries: list[datetime], tz: str, created: datetime) -> None:
   170	        """
   171	        `boundaries` is a list of timezone-aware datetimes of the starts of time
   172	        intervals (months or weeks), and should be pre-sorted in descending order.
   173	        """
   174	        self.records = []
   175	        prev_boundary = None
   176	        for b in boundaries:
   177	            # If the check was created *after* the start of the previous time
   178	            # interval then the check did not yet exist during this time interval:
   179	            no_data = prev_boundary is not None and created > prev_boundary
   180	            self.records.append(DowntimeRecord(b, tz, no_data, td(), 0))
   181	            prev_boundary = b
   182	
   183	    def add(self, when: datetime, duration: td) -> None:
   184	        for record in self.records:
   185	            if when >= record.boundary:
   186	                record.duration += duration
   187	                record.count += 1
   188	                return

   210	    failure_kw = models.CharField(max_length=200, blank=True)

   256	        if self.project_id and self.project.show_slugs:

   259	
   260	            # If ping_key is not set, use dummy placeholder

   276	
   277	    def email(self) -> str | None:
   278	        """Return check's ping email address in user's preferred style.
   279	
   280	        Note: this method reads self.project. If project is not loaded already,
   281	        this causes a SQL query.
   282	
   283	        """
   284	        if self.project_id and self.project.show_slugs:
   285	            if not self.slug:
   286	                return None
   287	
   288	            # If ping_key is not set, use dummy placeholder

   294	    def clamped_last_duration(self) -> td | None:
   295	        if self.last_duration and self.last_duration < MAX_DURATION:
   296	            return self.last_duration
   297	        return None
   298	
   299	    def get_grace_start(self, *, with_started: bool = True) -> datetime | None:
   300	        """Return the datetime when the grace period starts.
   301	
   302	        If the check is currently new, paused or down, return None.
   303	        """
   304	        # NEVER is a constant sentinel value (year 3000).
   305	        # Using None instead would make the min() logic clunky.
   306	        result = NEVER
   307	
   308	        if self.kind == "simple" and self.status == "up":
   309	            assert self.last_ping is not None
   310	            result = self.last_ping + self.timeout
   311	        elif self.kind == "cron" and self.status == "up":
   312	            assert self.last_ping is not None
   313	            # The complex case, next ping is expected based on cron schedule.
   314	            # Don't convert to naive datetimes (and so avoid ambiguities around
   315	            # DST transitions). cronsim will handle the timezone-aware datetimes.
   316	            last_local = self.last_ping.astimezone(ZoneInfo(self.tz))
   317	            result = next(CronSim(self.schedule, last_local))
   318	            # Important: convert from the local timezone back to UTC.
   319	            # If the result is kept in the local timezone, adding
   320	            # a timedelta to it later (in `going_down_after` and in `get_status`)
   321	            # may yield incorrect results during DST transitions.
   322	            result = result.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   323	        elif self.kind == "oncalendar" and self.status == "up":
   324	            assert self.last_ping is not None
   325	            last_local = self.last_ping.astimezone(ZoneInfo(self.tz))
   326	            try:
   327	                result = next(OnCalendar(self.schedule, last_local))
   328	                # Same as for cron, convert back to UTC:
   329	                result = result.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   330	            except StopIteration:
   331	                result = NEVER
   332	
   333	        if with_started and self.last_start and self.status != "down":
   334	            result = min(result, self.last_start)
   335	
   336	        return result if result != NEVER else None
   337	
   338	    def going_down_after(self) -> datetime | None:
   339	        """Return the datetime when the check goes down.
   340	
   341	        If the check is new or paused, and not currently running, return None.
   342	        If the check is already down, also return None.
   343	        """
   344	        grace_start = self.get_grace_start()
   345	        if grace_start is not None:
   346	            return grace_start + self.grace
   347	
   348	        return None
   349	
   350	    @cached_property
   351	    def cached_status(self) -> str:
   352	        return self.get_status()
   353	
   354	    def get_status(self, *, with_started: bool = False) -> str:
   355	        """Return current status for display."""
   356	        frozen_now = now()
   357	
   358	        if self.last_start:
   359	            if frozen_now >= self.last_start + self.grace:
   360	                return "down"

The registry is explicit: `Channel.kind` maps strings to transport classes in `hc/api/models.py`. I’m now extracting each transport’s superclass, overridden methods, and uses of base helpers like `tmpl`, `last_ping`, `down_checks`, and `HttpTransport.post/request/put`.

  1121	    def send_sms_limit_notice(self, transport: str, message: str) -> None:
  1122	        profile = self.project.owner_profile
  1123	        ctx = {
  1124	            "recipient": self.phone.value,
  1125	            "owner_email": self.project.owner.email,
  1126	            "transport": transport,
  1127	            "limit": profile.sms_limit,
  1128	            "message": message,
  1129	        }
  1130	        emails.sms_limit(self.project.team_emails(), ctx)
  1131	
  1132	    @property
  1133	    def transport(self) -> transports.Transport:
  1134	        if self.kind not in TRANSPORTS:
  1135	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Unknown channel kind: {self.kind}")
  1136	
  1137	        label, cls = TRANSPORTS[self.kind]
  1138	        # import transport classes on first use, and cache in TRANSPORTS
  1139	        if isinstance(cls, str):
  1140	            modulename, classname = cls.rsplit(".", maxsplit=1)
  1141	            cls = getattr(import_module(modulename), classname)
  1142	            TRANSPORTS[self.kind] = (label, cls)
  1143	
  1144	        return cls(self)
  1145	
  1146	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, is_test: bool = False) -> str:
  1147	        if self.transport.is_noop(flip.new_status):
  1148	            return "no-op"
  1149	
  1150	        n = Notification(channel=self)
  1151	        if is_test:
  1152	            # When sending a test notification we leave the owner field null.
  1153	            # (the passed check is a dummy, unsaved Check instance)

    12	class Email(Transport):
    13	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    14	        if not self.channel.email_verified:
    15	            raise TransportError("Email not verified")
    16	
    17	        unsub_link = self.channel.get_unsub_link()
    18	
    19	        headers = {
    20	            "List-Unsubscribe": f"<{unsub_link}>",
    21	            "List-Unsubscribe-Post": "List-Unsubscribe=One-Click",
    22	            "X-Bounce-ID": sign_bounce_id(f"n.{notification.code}"),
    23	        }
    24	
    25	        # If this email address has an associated account,
    26	        # - include a summary of projects the account has access to
    27	        # - use their preferred time zone to format datetimes
    28	        # Otherwise, use the channel owner's preferred time zone.
    29	        try:
    30	            profile = Profile.objects.get(user__email=self.channel.email.value)
    31	            projects = list(profile.projects())
    32	        except Profile.DoesNotExist:
    33	            profile = Profile.objects.for_user(self.channel.project.owner)
    34	            projects = None
    35	
    36	        ping = self.last_ping(flip)
    37	        body_bytes = get_ping_body_bytes(ping)
    38	        subject, attachment = None, None
    39	        if ping is not None and ping.scheme == "email" and body_bytes:
    40	            attachment = email.message_from_bytes(body_bytes, policy=email.policy.SMTP)
    41	            # If this is a multipart message then drop message/rfc822 parts to
    42	            # avoid recursion issues with deep attachment-within-attachment stacks.
    43	            if attachment.is_multipart():
    44	                parts = attachment.get_payload()
    45	                # If is_multipart=True then get_payload() returns list[Message].
    46	                # Mypy does not know this, hence the assert.
    47	                assert isinstance(parts, list)
    48	                # use list() here so we don't mutate the same list we're iterating
    49	                for part in list(parts):
    50	                    if part.get_content_type() == "message/rfc822":
    51	                        parts.remove(part)
    52	
    53	            subject = attachment.get("subject", "")
    54	
    55	        ctx = {
    56	            "flip": flip,
    57	            "check": flip.owner,
    58	            "ping": ping,
    59	            "body": body_bytes.decode(errors="replace") if body_bytes else None,
    60	            "subject": subject,
    61	            "projects": projects,
    62	            "unsub_link": unsub_link,
    63	            "tz": profile.tz,
    64	            "ping_attached": attachment is not None,
    65	        }
    66	
    67	        emails.alert(self.channel.email.value, ctx, headers, attachment)
    68

    70	        if status == "down":
    71	            return not self.channel.email.notify_down
    72	        else:
    73	            return not self.channel.email.notify_up

    12	class GitHub(HttpTransport):
    13	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    14	        return status != "down"
    15	
    16	    @classmethod
    17	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
    18	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
    19	        permanent = False
    20	        if response.status_code == 410:
    21	            # If GitHub returns HTTP 410 assume this will never work again
    22	            permanent = True
    23	
    24	        raise TransportError(message, permanent=permanent)

    26	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    27	        if not settings.GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY:
    28	            raise TransportError("GitHub notifications are not enabled.")
    29	
    30	        ping = self.last_ping(flip)
    31	        ctx = {
    32	            "flip": flip,
    33	            "check": flip.owner,
    34	            "status": flip.new_status,
    35	            "ping": ping,
    36	        }
    37	
    38	        body = get_ping_body(ping, maxlen=1000)
    39	        if body and "```" not in body:
    40	            ctx["body"] = body
    41	
    42	        url = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{self.channel.github.repo}/issues"
    43	        payload = {
    44	            "title": self.tmpl("github_title.html", **ctx),
    45	            "body": self.tmpl("github_body.html", **ctx),
    46	            "labels": self.channel.github.labels,
    47	        }
    48	
    49	        inst_id = self.channel.github.installation_id
    50	        token = client.get_installation_access_token(inst_id)
    51	        if token is None:
    52	            raise TransportError(f"GitHub denied access to {self.channel.github.repo}")
    53	
    54	        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    55	        self.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers)

    10	class Gotify(HttpTransport):
    11	    def priority_for_status(self, status: str) -> int | None:
    12	        cfg = self.channel.gotify
    13	        return cfg.priority_up if status == "up" else cfg.priority
    14	
    15	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    16	        return self.priority_for_status(status) == 0
    17	
    18	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    19	        base = self.channel.gotify.url
    20	        if not base.endswith("/"):
    21	            base += "/"
    22	
    23	        url = urljoin(base, "message")
    24	        url += "?" + urlencode({"token": self.channel.gotify.token})
    25	
    26	        ctx = {
    27	            "flip": flip,
    28	            "check": flip.owner,
    29	            "status": flip.new_status,
    30	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(flip.owner),
    31	        }
    32	        payload: JSONDict = {
    33	            "title": self.tmpl("gotify_title.html", **ctx),
    34	            "message": self.tmpl("gotify_message.html", **ctx),
    35	            "extras": {
    36	                "client::display": {"contentType": "text/markdown"},
    37	            },
    38	        }
    39	
    40	        priority = self.priority_for_status(flip.new_status)
    41	        if priority is not None:
    42	            payload["priority"] = priority
    43	
    44	        self.post(url, json=payload)

     7	class Group(Transport):
     8	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
     9	        channels = self.channel.group_channels
    10	        # If notification's owner field is None then this is a test notification,
    11	        # and we should pass is_test=True to channel.notify() calls
    12	        is_test = notification.owner is None
    13	        error_count = 0
    14	        for channel in channels:
    15	            error = channel.notify(flip, is_test=is_test)
    16	            if error and error != "no-op":
    17	                error_count += 1
    18	        if error_count:
    19	            raise TransportError(
    20	                f"{error_count} out of {len(channels)} notifications failed"
    21	            )

    11	class Matrix(HttpTransport):

    14	
    15	        assert isinstance(settings.MATRIX_HOMESERVER, str)
    16	        url = settings.MATRIX_HOMESERVER
    17	        url += f"/_matrix/client/v3/rooms/{room_id}/send/m.room.message/{uuid4()}"

    25	            "status": flip.new_status,
    26	            "ping": ping,
    27	            "body": get_ping_body(ping, maxlen=1000),
    28	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(flip.owner),
    29	        }
    30	        plain = self.tmpl("matrix_description.html", **ctx)
    31	        formatted = self.tmpl("matrix_description_formatted.html", **ctx)
    32	        payload = {
    33	            "msgtype": "m.text",

    37	        }
    38	        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {settings.MATRIX_ACCESS_TOKEN}"}
    39	        self.put(self.get_url(), json=payload, headers=headers)

     9	class Ntfy(HttpTransport):
    10	    def priority(self, status: str) -> int:
    11	        if status == "up":
    12	            return self.channel.ntfy.priority_up
    13	        return self.channel.ntfy.priority
    14	
    15	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    16	        return self.priority(status) == 0
    17	
    18	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    19	        ntfy = self.channel.ntfy
    20	        from hc.api.models import TokenBucket
    21	
    22	        if not TokenBucket.authorize_ntfy(ntfy.url, ntfy.topic):
    23	            raise TransportError("Rate limit exceeded")
    24	
    25	        ctx = {
    26	            "flip": flip,
    27	            "check": flip.owner,
    28	            "status": flip.new_status,
    29	            "ping": self.last_ping(flip),
    30	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(flip.owner),
    31	        }
    32	        payload = {

    15	class Pushover(HttpTransport):

    19	    class ErrorModel(BaseModel):

    21	
    22	    @classmethod
    23	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
    24	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
    25	        permanent = False
    26	        if response.status_code == 400:
    27	            try:
    28	                doc = Pushover.ErrorModel.model_validate_json(response.content)
    29	                if doc.user == "invalid":
    30	                    message += " (invalid user)"
    31	                    permanent = True
    32	            except ValidationError:
    33	                logger.debug("Pushover HTTP 400 with body: %s", response.content)

    37	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    38	        pieces = self.channel.value.split("|")
    39	        _, prio = pieces[0], pieces[1]
    40	
    41	        # The third element, if present, is the priority for "up" events
    42	        if status == "up" and len(pieces) == 3:
    43	            prio = pieces[2]
    44	
    45	        return int(prio) == -3
    46	
    47	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    48	        if not settings.PUSHOVER_API_TOKEN:
    49	            raise TransportError("Pushover notifications are not enabled.")
    50	
    51	        pieces = self.channel.value.split("|")
    52	        user_key, down_prio = pieces[0], pieces[1]
    53	
    54	        # The third element, if present, is the priority for "up" events
    55	        up_prio = down_prio
    56	        if len(pieces) == 3:
    57	            up_prio = pieces[2]
    58	
    59	        from hc.api.models import TokenBucket
    60	
    61	        if not TokenBucket.authorize_pushover(user_key):
    62	            raise TransportError("Rate limit exceeded")
    63	
    64	        check = flip.owner
    65	        # If down events have the emergency priority,
    66	        # send a cancel call first
    67	        if flip.new_status == "up" and down_prio == "2":
    68	            url = self.CANCEL_TMPL % check.unique_key
    69	            cancel_payload = {"token": settings.PUSHOVER_API_TOKEN}
    70	            self.post(url, data=cancel_payload)
    71	
    72	        ctx = {
    73	            "flip": flip,
    74	            "check": check,
    75	            "status": flip.new_status,
    76	            "ping": self.last_ping(flip),
    77	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(check),
    78	        }
    79	        text = self.tmpl("pushover_message.html", **ctx)
    80	        title = self.tmpl("pushover_title.html", **ctx)
    81	        prio = up_prio if flip.new_status == "up" else down_prio
    82	
    83	        payload = {
    84	            "token": settings.PUSHOVER_API_TOKEN,
    85	            "user": user_key,
    86	            "message": text,
    87	            "title": title,
    88	            "html": 1,
    89	            "priority": int(prio),

    92	            "url_title": f"View on {settings.SITE_NAME}",
    93	        }
    94	
    95	        # Emergency notification
    96	        if prio == "2":
    97	            payload["retry"] = settings.PUSHOVER_EMERGENCY_RETRY_DELAY
    98	            payload["expire"] = settings.PUSHOVER_EMERGENCY_EXPIRATION
    99

    13	class Shell(Transport):
    14	    def prepare(self, template: str, flip: Flip) -> str:
    15	        """Replace placeholders with actual values."""
    16	
    17	        check = flip.owner
    18	        ctx = {
    19	            "$CODE": str(check.code),
    20	            "$STATUS": flip.new_status,
    21	            "$NOW": flip.created.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat(),
    22	            "$NAME": shlex.quote(check.name),
    23	            "$TAGS": shlex.quote(check.tags),
    24	        }
    25	
    26	        for i, tag in enumerate(check.tags_list()):

    28

    31	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    32	        if status == "down" and not self.channel.shell.cmd_down:
    33	            return True
    34	
    35	        if status == "up" and not self.channel.shell.cmd_up:
    36	            return True
    37	
    38	        return False
    39	
    40	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    41	        if not settings.SHELL_ENABLED:
    42	            raise TransportError("Shell commands are not enabled")
    43	
    44	        if flip.new_status == "up":
    45	            cmd = self.channel.shell.cmd_up
    46	        elif flip.new_status == "down":
    47	            cmd = self.channel.shell.cmd_down
    48	
    49	        cmd = self.prepare(cmd, flip)
    50	        code = os.system(cmd)
    51	
    52	        if code != 0:
    53	            raise TransportError(f"Command returned exit code {code}")

    19	class SignalRateLimitFailure(TransportError):
    20	    def __init__(self, token: str, reply: bytes):

    26	class Signal(Transport):
    27	    TIMEOUT = 60
    28	
    29	    class Result(BaseModel):
    30	        type: str
    31	        token: str | None = None
    32	
    33	    class Response(BaseModel):
    34	        results: list[Signal.Result]
    35	
    36	    class Data(BaseModel):
    37	        response: Signal.Response
    38	
    39	    class Error(BaseModel):
    40	        code: int
    41	        data: Signal.Data | None = None
    42	
    43	    class Reply(BaseModel):
    44	        id: str = ""
    45	        error: Signal.Error | None = None
    46	
    47	        def get_results(self) -> list[Signal.Result]:
    48	            assert self.error
    49	            if self.error.data is None:
    50	                return []
    51	            return self.error.data.response.results
    52	
    53	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    54	        if status == "down":
    55	            return not self.channel.phone.notify_down
    56	        else:
    57	            return not self.channel.phone.notify_up
    58	
    59	    @classmethod
    60	    def send(cls, recipient: str, message: str) -> None:
    61	        plaintext, styles = extract_signal_styles(message)
    62	        if "." in recipient:
    63	            # usernames must be prefixed with "u:"
    64	            recipient = f"u:{recipient}"
    65	        payload = {
    66	            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    67	            "method": "send",
    68	            "params": {
    69	                "recipient": [recipient],
    70	                "message": plaintext,
    71	                "textStyle": styles,
    72	            },
    73	            "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    74	        }
    75	
    76	        payload_bytes = (json.dumps(payload) + "\n").encode()
    77	        for reply_bytes in cls._read_replies(payload_bytes):
    78	            try:
    79	                reply = Signal.Reply.model_validate_json(reply_bytes)
    80	            except ValidationError:
    81	                logger.error("unexpected signal-cli response: %s", reply_bytes)
    82	                raise TransportError("signal-cli call failed (unexpected response)")
    83	
    84	            if reply.id != payload["id"]:
    85	                continue
    86	
    87	            if reply.error is None:
    88	                break  # success!
    89	
    90	            for result in reply.get_results():
    91	                if result.type == "UNREGISTERED_FAILURE":
    92	                    raise TransportError("Recipient not found", permanent=True)
    93	
    94	                if result.type == "RATE_LIMIT_FAILURE" and result.token:
    95	                    raise SignalRateLimitFailure(result.token, reply_bytes)
    96	
    97	            msg = f"signal-cli call failed ({reply.error.code})"
    98	            msg_with_extras = f"{msg} for {recipient}\n{reply_bytes.decode()}"
    99	            # Include signal-cli reply in the message we log for ourselves
   100	            logger.error(msg_with_extras)
   101	            # Do not include signal-cli reply in the message we show to the user
   102	            raise TransportError(msg)
   103	
   104	    @classmethod
   105	    def _read_replies(cls, payload_bytes: bytes) -> Iterator[bytes]:
   106	        """Send a request to signal-cli over UNIX socket. Read and yield replies.
   107	
   108	        This method:
   109	        * opens UNIX socket
   110	        * sends the request data (JSON RPC data encoded as bytes)
   111	        * reads newline-terminated responses and yields them
   112	
   113	        Individual sendall and recv operations have a timeout of 15 seconds.
   114	        This method also keeps track of total time spent in the method, and raises
   115	        an exception when the total time exceeds 15 seconds.
   116	
   117	        """
   118	
   119	        if not settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET:
   120	            raise TransportError("Signal notifications are not enabled")
   121	
   122	        start = time.time()
   123	        address: str | tuple[str, int]
   124	        if ":" in settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET:
   125	            stype = socket.AF_INET
   126	            parts = settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET.split(":")
   127	            address = (parts[0], int(parts[1]))
   128	        else:
   129	            stype = socket.AF_UNIX
   130	            address = settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET
   131	
   132	        with socket.socket(stype, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
   133	            s.settimeout(cls.TIMEOUT)
   134	            try:
   135	                s.connect(address)
   136	                s.sendall(payload_bytes)
   137	                s.shutdown(socket.SHUT_WR)  # we are done sending
   138	
   139	                buffer = []
   140	                while True:
   141	                    ch = s.recv(1)
   142	                    buffer.append(ch)
   143	                    if ch in (b"\n", b""):
   144	                        yield b"".join(buffer)
   145	                        buffer = []
   146	
   147	                    if time.time() - start > cls.TIMEOUT:
   148	                        raise TransportError("signal-cli call timed out")
   149	
   150	            except OSError as e:
   151	                msg = f"signal-cli call failed ({e})"
   152	                # Log the exception, so any configured logging handlers can pick it up
   153	                logger.exception(msg)
   154	
   155	                # And then report it the same as other errors
   156	                raise TransportError(msg)
   157	
   158	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
   159	        if not settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET:
   160	            raise TransportError("Signal notifications are not enabled")
   161	
   162	        from hc.api.models import TokenBucket
   163	
   164	        if not TokenBucket.authorize_signal(self.channel.phone.value):
   165	            raise TransportError("Rate limit exceeded")
   166	
   167	        ctx = {
   168	            "flip": flip,
   169	            "check": flip.owner,
   170	            "status": flip.new_status,
   171	            "ping": self.last_ping(flip),
   172	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(flip.owner),
   173	        }
   174	        text = self.tmpl("signal_message.html", **ctx)
   175	        tries_left = 2
   176	        while True:
   177	            try:
   178	                return self.send(self.channel.phone.value, text)
   179	            except SignalRateLimitFailure as e:
   180	                self.channel.send_signal_captcha_alert(e.token, e.reply.decode())
   181	                plaintext, _ = extract_signal_styles(text)
   182	                self.channel.send_signal_rate_limited_notice(text, plaintext)
   183	                raise e
   184	            except TransportError as e:
   185	                tries_left -= 1
   186	                if e.permanent or tries_left == 0:
   187	                    raise e
   188	                logger.debug("Retrying signal-cli call")

    28	class Slackalike(HttpTransport):
    29	    """Base class for transports that use Slack-compatible incoming webhooks."""
    30	
    31	    def payload(self, flip: Flip) -> JSONDict:
    32	        """Prepare JSON-serializable payload for Slack-compatible incoming webhook."""
    33	        check = flip.owner
    34	        name = check.name_then_code()
    35	        fields = SlackFields()
    36	        text = None
    37	        if flip.reason:
    38	            text = f"Reason: {flip.reason_long()}." if flip.reason else None
    39	        elif flip.new_status == "up" and flip.down_duration:
    40	            formatted_duration = format_duration_for_sentence(flip.down_duration)
    41	            text = f"The downtime lasted {formatted_duration}."
    42	
    43	        result: JSONDict = {
    44	            "username": settings.SITE_NAME,
    45	            "icon_url": absolute_site_logo_url(),
    46	            "attachments": [
    47	                {
    48	                    "color": "good" if flip.new_status == "up" else "danger",
    49	                    "fallback": f'The check "{name}" is {flip.new_status.upper()}.',
    50	                    "mrkdwn_in": ["fields"],
    51	                    "title": f"“{name}” is {flip.new_status.upper()}.",
    52	                    "title_link": check.cloaked_url(),
    53	                    "text": text,
    54	                    "fields": fields,
    55	                }
    56	            ],
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        if check.desc:
    60	            fields.add("Description", check.desc, short=False)
    61	
    62	        if check.project.name:
    63	            fields.add("Project", check.project.name)
    64	
    65	        if tags := check.tags_list():
    66	            fields.add("Tags", " ".join(f"`{tag}`" for tag in tags))
    67	
    68	        if check.kind == "simple":
    69	            fields.add("Period", format_duration(check.timeout))
    70	
    71	        if check.kind in ("cron", "oncalendar"):
    72	            fields.add("Schedule", self.fix_asterisks(check.schedule))
    73	            fields.add("Time Zone", check.tz)
    74	
    75	        if ping := self.last_ping(flip):
    76	            fields.add("Total Pings", str(ping.n))
    77	            fields.add("Last Ping", ping.formatted_kind_created())
    78	        else:
    79	            fields.add("Total Pings", "0")
    80	            fields.add("Last Ping", "Never")
    81	
    82	        body = get_ping_body(ping, maxlen=1000)
    83	        if body and "```" not in body:
    84	            fields.add("Last Ping Body", f"```\n{body}\n```", short=False)
    85	
    86	        return result

    88	    def fix_asterisks(self, s: str) -> str:
    89	        """Escape asterisks so that they are not recognized as Markdown syntax."""
    90	
    91	        # The base implementation prepends asterisks with "Combining Grapheme Joiner"
    92	        # characters but subclasses can override this function and escape
    93	        # asterisks differently
    94	        return s.replace("*", "\u034f*")

    96	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    97	        self.post(self.channel.slack_webhook_url, json=self.payload(flip))

   100	class Slack(Slackalike):
   101	    @classmethod
   102	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
   103	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
   104	        permanent = False
   105	        if response.status_code == 404:
   106	            # If Slack returns 404, this endpoint is unlikely to ever work again
   107	            # https://api.slack.com/messaging/webhooks#handling_errors
   108	            permanent = True
   109	        elif response.status_code == 400:
   110	            if response.content == b"invalid_token":
   111	                # If Slack returns 400 with "invalid_token" in response body,
   112	                # we're using a deactivated user's token to post to a private channel.
   113	                # In theory this condition can recover (a deactivated user can be
   114	                # activated), but in practice it is unlikely to happen.
   115	                permanent = True
   116	            else:
   117	                # Log it for later inspection
   118	                logger.debug("Slack returned HTTP 400 with body: %s", response.content)
   119	
   120	        raise TransportError(message, permanent=permanent)
   121	
   122	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
   123	        if not settings.SLACK_ENABLED:
   124	            raise TransportError("Slack notifications are not enabled.")
   125	
   126	        self.post(self.channel.slack_webhook_url, json=self.payload(flip))

    12	class MigrationRequiredError(TransportError):
    13	    def __init__(self, message: str, new_chat_id: int):

    15	        self.new_chat_id = new_chat_id

    18	class Telegram(HttpTransport):
    19	    SM = f"https://api.telegram.org/bot{settings.TELEGRAM_TOKEN}/sendMessage"
    20	
    21	    class MigrationParameters(BaseModel):
    22	        migrate_to_chat_id: int
    23	
    24	    class ErrorModel(BaseModel):
    25	        description: str
    26	        parameters: Telegram.MigrationParameters | None = None
    27	
    28	    @classmethod
    29	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
    30	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
    31	        try:
    32	            m = Telegram.ErrorModel.model_validate_json(response.content)
    33	        except ValidationError:
    34	            raise TransportError(message)
    35	
    36	        if m.parameters:
    37	            # If the error payload contains the migrate_to_chat_id field,
    38	            # raise MigrationRequiredError, with the new chat_id included
    39	            chat_id = m.parameters.migrate_to_chat_id
    40	            raise MigrationRequiredError(m.description, chat_id)
    41	
    42	        permanent = False
    43	        message += f' with a message: "{m.description}"'
    44	        if m.description == "Forbidden: the group chat was deleted":
    45	            permanent = True
    46	        elif m.description == "Forbidden: bot was blocked by the user":
    47	            permanent = True
    48	        elif m.description == "Forbidden: user is deactivated":
    49	            permanent = True
    50	
    51	        raise TransportError(message, permanent=permanent)
    52	
    53	    @classmethod
    54	    def send(cls, chat_id: int, thread_id: int | None, text: str) -> None:
    55	        # Telegram.send is a separate method because it is also used in
    56	        # hc.front.views.telegram_bot to send invite links.
    57	        payload = {
    58	            "chat_id": chat_id,
    59	            "message_thread_id": thread_id,
    60	            "text": text,
    61	            "parse_mode": "html",
    62	        }
    63	        cls.post(cls.SM, json=payload)
    64	
    65	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    66	        from hc.api.models import TokenBucket
    67	
    68	        if not TokenBucket.authorize_telegram(self.channel.telegram.id):
    69	            raise TransportError("Rate limit exceeded")
    70	
    71	        ping = self.last_ping(flip)
    72	        ctx = {
    73	            "flip": flip,
    74	            "check": flip.owner,
    75	            "status": flip.new_status,
    76	            "down_checks": self.down_checks(flip.owner),
    77	            "ping": ping,
    78	            # Telegram's message limit is 4096 chars, but clip body at 1000 for
    79	            # consistency
    80	            "body": get_ping_body(ping, maxlen=1000),
    81	        }
    82	        text = self.tmpl("telegram_message.html", **ctx)
    83	
    84	        try:
    85	            self.send(self.channel.telegram.id, self.channel.telegram.thread_id, text)
    86	        except MigrationRequiredError as e:
    87	            # Save the new chat_id, then try sending again:
    88	            self.channel.update_telegram_id(e.new_chat_id)
    89	            self.send(self.channel.telegram.id, self.channel.telegram.thread_id, text)

    12	class Webhook(HttpTransport):
    13	    def prepare(
    14	        self,
    15	        template: str,
    16	        flip: Flip,
    17	        urlencode: bool = False,
    18	        latin1: bool = False,
    19	        allow_ping_body: bool = False,
    20	    ) -> str:
    21	        """Replace variables with actual values."""
    22	
    23	        def safe(s: str) -> str:
    24	            return quote(s) if urlencode else s
    25	
    26	        check = flip.owner
    27	        ctx = {
    28	            "$CODE": str(check.code),
    29	            "$STATUS": flip.new_status,
    30	            "$NOW": safe(flip.created.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()),
    31	            "$NAME_JSON": safe(json.dumps(check.name)),
    32	            "$NAME": safe(check.name),
    33	            "$SLUG": check.slug,
    34	            "$TAGS": safe(check.tags),
    35	            "$JSON": safe(json.dumps(check.to_dict())),
    36	        }
    37	
    38	        # Materialize ping body only if template refers to it.
    39	        if allow_ping_body and "$BODY" in template:
    40	            body = get_ping_body(self.last_ping(flip))
    41	            ctx["$BODY_JSON"] = json.dumps(body if body else "")
    42	            ctx["$BODY"] = body if body else ""
    43	
    44	        if "$EXITSTATUS" in template:
    45	            ctx["$EXITSTATUS"] = "-1"
    46	            lp = self.last_ping(flip)
    47	            if lp and lp.exitstatus is not None:
    48	                ctx["$EXITSTATUS"] = str(lp.exitstatus)
    49	
    50	        for i, tag in enumerate(check.tags_list()):
    51	            ctx["$TAG%d" % (i + 1)] = safe(tag)
    52	
    53	        result = replace(template, ctx)
    54	        if latin1:
    55	            # Replace non-latin-1 characters with XML character references.
    56	            result = result.encode("latin-1", "xmlcharrefreplace").decode("latin-1")
    57	
    58	        return result
    59	
    60	    def is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool:
    61	        spec = self.channel.webhook_spec(status)
    62	        if not spec.url:
    63	            return True
    64	
    65	        return False
    66	
    67	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    68	        if not settings.WEBHOOKS_ENABLED:
    69	            raise TransportError("Webhook notifications are not enabled.")
    70	
    71	        spec = self.channel.webhook_spec(flip.new_status)
    72	        if not spec.url:
    73	            raise TransportError("Empty webhook URL")
    74	
    75	        method = spec.method.lower()
    76	        url = self.prepare(spec.url, flip, urlencode=True)
    77	        retry = True
    78	        if notification.owner is None:
    79	            # This is a test notification.
    80	            # When sending a test notification, don't retry on failures.
    81	            retry = False
    82	
    83	        body, body_bytes = spec.body, None
    84	        if body and spec.method in ("POST", "PUT"):
    85	            body = self.prepare(body, flip, allow_ping_body=True)
    86	            body_bytes = body.encode()
    87	
    88	        headers = {}
    89	        for key, value in spec.headers.items():
    90	            # Header values should contain ASCII and latin-1 only
    91	            headers[key] = self.prepare(value, flip, latin1=True)
    92	
    93	        self.request(method, url, retry=retry, data=body_bytes, headers=headers)

    18	class SlackFields(list[JSONValue]):
    19	    """Helper class for preparing [{"title": ..., "value": ... }, ...] structures."""
    20	
    21	    def add(self, title: str, value: str, short: bool = True) -> None:
    22	        field: JSONDict = {"title": title, "value": value}
    23	        if short:
    24	            field["short"] = True
    25	        self.append(field)
    26

    28	class Slackalike(HttpTransport):
    29	    """Base class for transports that use Slack-compatible incoming webhooks."""
    30	
    31	    def payload(self, flip: Flip) -> JSONDict:
    32	        """Prepare JSON-serializable payload for Slack-compatible incoming webhook."""
    33	        check = flip.owner
    34	        name = check.name_then_code()
    35	        fields = SlackFields()
    36	        text = None
    37	        if flip.reason:
    38	            text = f"Reason: {flip.reason_long()}." if flip.reason else None
    39	        elif flip.new_status == "up" and flip.down_duration:
    40	            formatted_duration = format_duration_for_sentence(flip.down_duration)
    41	            text = f"The downtime lasted {formatted_duration}."
    42	
    43	        result: JSONDict = {
    44	            "username": settings.SITE_NAME,
    45	            "icon_url": absolute_site_logo_url(),
    46	            "attachments": [
    47	                {
    48	                    "color": "good" if flip.new_status == "up" else "danger",
    49	                    "fallback": f'The check "{name}" is {flip.new_status.upper()}.',
    50	                    "mrkdwn_in": ["fields"],
    51	                    "title": f"“{name}” is {flip.new_status.upper()}.",
    52	                    "title_link": check.cloaked_url(),
    53	                    "text": text,
    54	                    "fields": fields,
    55	                }
    56	            ],
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        if check.desc:
    60	            fields.add("Description", check.desc, short=False)
    61	
    62	        if check.project.name:
    63	            fields.add("Project", check.project.name)
    64	
    65	        if tags := check.tags_list():
    66	            fields.add("Tags", " ".join(f"`{tag}`" for tag in tags))
    67	
    68	        if check.kind == "simple":
    69	            fields.add("Period", format_duration(check.timeout))
    70	
    71	        if check.kind in ("cron", "oncalendar"):
    72	            fields.add("Schedule", self.fix_asterisks(check.schedule))
    73	            fields.add("Time Zone", check.tz)
    74	
    75	        if ping := self.last_ping(flip):
    76	            fields.add("Total Pings", str(ping.n))
    77	            fields.add("Last Ping", ping.formatted_kind_created())
    78	        else:
    79	            fields.add("Total Pings", "0")
    80	            fields.add("Last Ping", "Never")
    81	
    82	        body = get_ping_body(ping, maxlen=1000)
    83	        if body and "```" not in body:
    84	            fields.add("Last Ping Body", f"```\n{body}\n```", short=False)
    85	
    86	        return result
    87	
    88	    def fix_asterisks(self, s: str) -> str:
    89	        """Escape asterisks so that they are not recognized as Markdown syntax."""
    90	
    91	        # The base implementation prepends asterisks with "Combining Grapheme Joiner"
    92	        # characters but subclasses can override this function and escape
    93	        # asterisks differently
    94	        return s.replace("*", "\u034f*")
    95	
    96	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
    97	        self.post(self.channel.slack_webhook_url, json=self.payload(flip))

   100	class Slack(Slackalike):
   101	    @classmethod
   102	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
   103	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
   104	        permanent = False
   105	        if response.status_code == 404:
   106	            # If Slack returns 404, this endpoint is unlikely to ever work again
   107	            # https://api.slack.com/messaging/webhooks#handling_errors
   108	            permanent = True
   109	        elif response.status_code == 400:
   110	            if response.content == b"invalid_token":
   111	                # If Slack returns 400 with "invalid_token" in response body,
   112	                # we're using a deactivated user's token to post to a private channel.
   113	                # In theory this condition can recover (a deactivated user can be
   114	                # activated), but in practice it is unlikely to happen.
   115	                permanent = True
   116	            else:
   117	                # Log it for later inspection
   118	                logger.debug("Slack returned HTTP 400 with body: %s", response.content)
   119	
   120	        raise TransportError(message, permanent=permanent)
   121	
   122	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
   123	        if not settings.SLACK_ENABLED:
   124	            raise TransportError("Slack notifications are not enabled.")
   125	
   126	        self.post(self.channel.slack_webhook_url, json=self.payload(flip))

   104	    @classmethod
   105	    def _read_replies(cls, payload_bytes: bytes) -> Iterator[bytes]:
   106	        """Send a request to signal-cli over UNIX socket. Read and yield replies.
   107	
   108	        This method:
   109	        * opens UNIX socket
   110	        * sends the request data (JSON RPC data encoded as bytes)
   111	        * reads newline-terminated responses and yields them
   112	
   113	        Individual sendall and recv operations have a timeout of 15 seconds.
   114	        This method also keeps track of total time spent in the method, and raises
   115	        an exception when the total time exceeds 15 seconds.
   116	
   117	        """
   118	
   119	        if not settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET:
   120	            raise TransportError("Signal notifications are not enabled")
   121	
   122	        start = time.time()
   123	        address: str | tuple[str, int]
   124	        if ":" in settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET:
   125	            stype = socket.AF_INET
   126	            parts = settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET.split(":")
   127	            address = (parts[0], int(parts[1]))
   128	        else:
   129	            stype = socket.AF_UNIX
   130	            address = settings.SIGNAL_CLI_SOCKET
   131	
   132	        with socket.socket(stype, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
   133	            s.settimeout(cls.TIMEOUT)
   134	            try:
   135	                s.connect(address)
   136	                s.sendall(payload_bytes)
   137	                s.shutdown(socket.SHUT_WR)  # we are done sending
   138	
   139	                buffer = []
   140	                while True:
   141	                    ch = s.recv(1)
   142	                    buffer.append(ch)
   143	                    if ch in (b"\n", b""):
   144	                        yield b"".join(buffer)
   145	                        buffer = []
   146	
   147	                    if time.time() - start > cls.TIMEOUT:
   148	                        raise TransportError("signal-cli call timed out")
   149	
   150	            except OSError as e:
   151	                msg = f"signal-cli call failed ({e})"
   152	                # Log the exception, so any configured logging handlers can pick it up
   153	                logger.exception(msg)
   154	
   155	                # And then report it the same as other errors
   156	                raise TransportError(msg)

    28	    # connection. The if condition makes sure this does not run during tests.

    "project": "Healthchecks is a Django project. The repo root has manage.py, templates/, static assets, and the Python package under hc/.",
    "organization": [
      "hc/api: core check, channel, flip, notification models plus alert sending commands.",
      "hc/accounts: users, projects, profiles, account-related management commands.",
      "hc/front: web UI views/forms/templates helpers.",
      "hc/integrations/<kind>: one package per notification integration, usually with transport.py, views.py/forms.py, and tests.",
      "hc/lib: shared infrastructure helpers such as curl, email, S3, statsd, dates."
    ],
    "base_contract_location": "hc/api/transports.py:60 defines Transport, the base notification-delivery contract. hc/api/transports.py:130 defines HttpTransport, the HTTP-oriented intermediate base used by most integrations.",
    "dispatch_path": "hc/api/models.py:47 defines TRANSPORTS, the string registry from Channel.kind to transport class. hc/api/models.py:1133 loads Channel.transport through that registry, and hc/api/models.py:1146 Channel.notify creates a Notification and calls self.transport.notify(flip, notification=n)."
  },
  "step_2_contract": {
    "base_class": "hc/api/transports.py:60 class Transport",
    "entry_point": "hc/api/transports.py:64 Transport.notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None; concrete transports raise TransportError on failure and return None on success.",
    "pre_entry_filter": "hc/api/transports.py:74 Transport.is_noop(self, status: str) -> bool; Channel.notify calls it before creating/sending a Notification at hc/api/models.py:1147.",
    "shared_helpers": [
      "hc/api/transports.py:85 Transport.down_checks(check): used by message transports to include other down checks.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:103 Transport.last_ping(flip): used by many transports to attach/contextualize the last ping.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:117 Transport.tmpl(template_name, **ctx): template rendering helper used broadly for message bodies/titles.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:130 HttpTransport(Transport): HTTP-specific intermediate base.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:132 HttpTransport.raise_for_response(response): overridden by HTTP transports that need permanent-error handling or vendor error parsing.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:137 HttpTransport._request(...): wraps hc.lib.curl.request and maps curl errors/statuses to TransportError.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:165 HttpTransport.request(..., retry): retry loop; Webhook calls this directly.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:198 HttpTransport.post(...): convenience wrapper used by most HTTP transports.",
      "hc/api/transports.py:222 HttpTransport.put(...): convenience wrapper used by Matrix."
    ],
    "contract_notes": [
      "Changing Transport.notify signature requires updating all 29 registered concrete transports and Channel.notify at hc/api/models.py:1167.",
      "Changing is_noop must preserve Channel.notify semantics: returning \"no-op\" at hc/api/models.py:1147 before Notification creation.",
      "Changing TransportError shape affects Channel.notify error persistence and permanent disable behavior at hc/api/models.py:1169.",
      "Changing tmpl, last_ping, or down_checks reaches many implementations even when their notify signature remains compatible.",
      "Changing HttpTransport request/post/put/raise_for_response reaches most registered transports because they subclass HttpTransport directly or through Slackalike."
    ]
  },
